title: "Deployment & Operations"

## Environment Strategy

```
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    Environment Topology                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+

Development:
   |-> Local machines
   |-> Local PostgreSQL
   |-> Mock external services
   +-> Seed data

Staging:
   |-> Render.com
   |-> Supabase staging project
   |-> Full integration
   |-> Production-like config
   +-> QA testing

Production:
   |-> Render.com
   |-> Supabase production
   |-> Auto-scaling
   |-> High availability
   +-> Monitoring active
```


## Deployment Pipeline

```
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
|                CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)              |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+

Code Push to main
        |
        |-> 1. Run Tests
        |   |-> Unit tests (pytest)
        |   |-> Integration tests
        |   |-> Coverage (>80%)
        |   +-> Security scan (Bandit)
        |
        |-> 2. Build
        |   |-> Docker image
        |   |-> Dependency resolution
        |   +-> Static analysis (mypy)
        |
        |-> 3. Deploy to Staging
        |   |-> Database migrations
        |   |-> Deploy container
        |   |-> Smoke tests
        |   +-> Wait for approval
        |
        +-> 4. Deploy to Production
            |-> Blue-green deployment
            |-> Database migrations
            |-> Health checks
            |-> Gradual traffic shift
            +-> Rollback on failure
```

### GitHub Actions Workflow

```yaml
name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3

  deploy-staging:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to Render
        run: curl -X POST ${{ secrets.RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_STAGING }}

  deploy-production:
    needs: deploy-staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to Render
        run: curl -X POST ${{ secrets.RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_PROD }}
```


## Infrastructure Components

### Hosting: Render.com

The `render.yaml` blueprint declares four services and one shared
environment group. Auto-deploy is disabled on all services;
deployments are triggered via deploy hooks from GitHub Actions.

| Service | Type | Runtime | Region |
|---------|------|---------|--------|
| identity-api | web | Python 3.12 | Frankfurt |
| identity-celery-worker | worker | Python 3.12 | Frankfurt |
| identity-redis | key-value | Redis | Frankfurt |
| identity-frontend | web (static) | Static (CDN) | Global |

Secrets are managed in the `identity-api-shared` environment group
and entered via the Render dashboard (`sync: false`). Non-secret
configuration values (algorithm, token expiry, log level) are
committed to `render.yaml`.

### Database: Supabase

- Managed PostgreSQL 15+
- Row-Level Security
- Automatic backups
- Connection pooling via Supavisor

### Caching: Redis (Render)

- Managed Redis instance on Render (starter plan with persistence)
- Used for JWT blacklist (fail-closed), Celery message broker
- Eviction policy: volatile-ttl (expires keys with TTL first)


## High Availability Design

```
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
|              High Availability Architecture                 |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+

                Load Balancer (Render)
                        |
        +---------------+---------------+
        |               |               |
   Instance #1     Instance #2     Instance #3
   (Active)        (Active)        (Active)
        |               |               |
        +---------------+---------------+
                        |
                  PostgreSQL
                  (Supabase)
                        |
                  +-----+-----+
              Primary    Read Replicas
              (RW)          (RO)

Failover:
   |-> API: Load balancer redirects
   |-> Database: Automatic promotion
   +-> RTO: < 5 minutes
```


## Monitoring Strategy

### Observability Stack

```
Metrics:
   |-> API response times (p50, p95, p99)
   |-> Request throughput
   |-> Error rates
   |-> Database performance
   +-> Resource utilization

Logs:
   |-> Application logs (JSON)
   |-> Audit logs (immutable)
   |-> Security logs
   +-> Error logs

Traces:
   |-> Request tracing
   |-> Database queries
   +-> External service calls

Alerts:
   |-> Error rate > 1% -> Page
   |-> Response time p95 > 500ms -> Warn
   |-> Database connections > 80% -> Warn
   +-> Security events -> Immediate
```

### Health Check Endpoint

```python
@app.get("/health")
async def health_check():
    return {
        "status": "healthy",
        "version": "1.0.0",
        "timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        "checks": {
            "database": await check_database(),
            "cache": await check_cache(),
            "auth": await check_auth_service()
        }
    }
```


## Logging Architecture

### Structured Logging

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2025-10-13T14:23:45.123Z",
  "level": "INFO",
  "service": "identity-api",
  "request_id": "req_abc123",
  "user_id": "user_xyz789",
  "endpoint": "/api/v1/users/xyz789/profile",
  "method": "GET",
  "status": 200,
  "duration_ms": 45,
  "ip_address": "192.168.1.1"
}
```

### Log Levels
- `DEBUG`: Development only
- `INFO`: Normal operations
- `WARNING`: Unusual conditions
- `ERROR`: Application errors
- `CRITICAL`: System failures

### Log Retention
- Application logs: 30 days
- Audit logs: 7 years
- Security logs: 2 years
- Debug logs: 7 days


## Backup and Recovery

### Backup Strategy

```
Database Backups:
   |-> Continuous PITR (Supabase)
   |-> Daily full backups
   |-> 30-day retention
   +-> Encrypted at rest

Configuration:
   |-> Infrastructure as Code (Git)
   |-> Environment variables (secrets)
   +-> Database schemas (migrations)

Disaster Recovery:
   |-> RPO: 1 hour
   |-> RTO: 4 hours
   +-> DR drills: Quarterly
```

### Recovery Procedures

```
1. Identify Failure
   +-> Monitoring alerts
   +-> Incident declared

2. Assess Impact
   +-> Affected services
   +-> Data loss estimate

3. Execute Recovery
   +-> Restore from backup
   +-> Replay transactions
   +-> Verify integrity

4. Post-Mortem
   +-> Root cause analysis
   +-> Prevention measures
```


## Maintenance Windows

### Scheduled Maintenance
- **Frequency**: Monthly (3rd Sunday)
- **Duration**: 2-hour window
- **Time**: 02:00-04:00 UTC
- **Activities**: Security patches, upgrades

### Emergency Maintenance
- Security vulnerabilities: Immediate
- Critical bugs: Within 24 hours
- Performance issues: Within 72 hours


## Scaling Strategy

### Horizontal Scaling (Scale Out)

```
100K Users:
   +-> 2-3 API instances
   +-> Current configuration sufficient

1M Users:
   |-> 5-10 API instances
   |-> Database read replicas
   |-> Aggressive caching
   +-> Queue-based processing
```

### Vertical Scaling (Scale Up)

```
When needed:
   |-> Increase instance size
   |-> More database compute
   +-> Before horizontal scaling
```

### Auto-Scaling Rules

```yaml
triggers:
  - metric: cpu_utilization
    threshold: 70%
    action: scale_up
  - metric: request_queue_depth
    threshold: 100
    action: scale_up
  - metric: cpu_utilization
    threshold: 30%
    action: scale_down
```


## Deployed Production Infrastructure

The production environment runs on Render.com with four services defined
in `render.yaml` as an Infrastructure-as-Code blueprint. The following
diagram depicts the production topology only; development uses local
Docker Compose with Supabase CLI as described in the Environment Strategy
section above.

```{mermaid}
graph TB
    Browser["Browser"] -->|HTTPS| Cloudflare

    subgraph CF["Cloudflare"]
        Cloudflare["DNS + CDN Edge<br/>identity.okrbusiness.com"]
    end

    subgraph Render["Render.com"]
        subgraph Frontend["Global CDN"]
            StaticSite["identity-frontend<br/>(Vue 3 SPA, static)"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Frankfurt Region"]
            API["identity-api<br/>(FastAPI, Python 3.12)"]
            Worker["identity-celery-worker<br/>(async emails)"]
            Redis["identity-redis<br/>(sessions, broker)"]
        end
    end

    subgraph External["External Services"]
        Supabase[("Supabase<br/>PostgreSQL 15+")]
        Google["Google OAuth"]
    end

    Cloudflare -->|CNAME| StaticSite
    StaticSite -->|API calls| API
    API --> Redis
    API --> Supabase
    Worker --> Redis
    Worker --> Supabase
    API -->|social auth| Google

    style CF fill:#f9e0c7
    style Frontend fill:#e3f2fd
    style Backend fill:#fff3e0
    style External fill:#f3e5f5
```

Backend services (API, Celery worker, Redis) run in the Frankfurt region.
The frontend static site deploys to Render's global CDN without a fixed
region, which reduces latency for geographically distributed users.

### Custom Domain

The frontend is accessible at `https://identity.okrbusiness.com` through
Cloudflare DNS. A CNAME record points to the Render-assigned hostname
(`identity-frontend-ka3l.onrender.com`). Render provisions and renews
TLS certificates automatically via Let's Encrypt. Cloudflare operates
in Full (strict) SSL mode, ensuring end-to-end encryption between
the browser, Cloudflare edge, and Render origin.

### Deployment Trigger

Deployments are not triggered by Render's auto-deploy mechanism.
Instead, a GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`)
fires on pushes to `main` that modify `backend/`, `frontend/`, or
`render.yaml`. The workflow calls Render deploy hooks stored as
GitHub repository secrets (`RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_API`,
`RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_WORKER`, `RENDER_DEPLOY_HOOK_FRONTEND`).
This approach provides path-based filtering, so backend-only changes
do not trigger a frontend rebuild and vice versa.

### Environment Configuration

Shared environment variables for backend services are managed through
the `identity-api-shared` Render environment group. Secrets
(`DATABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, etc.)
use `sync: false` and are entered via the Render dashboard.
Frontend environment variables (`VITE_API_BASE_URL`, `VITE_API_VERSION`)
are baked into the JavaScript bundle at build time by Vite and are not
runtime-configurable.


## Security Operations

### Security Monitoring
- Failed authentication attempts
- Authorization failures
- Unusual access patterns
- Data export requests
- Admin operations

### Incident Response Plan

```
1. Detection
   +-> Automated alerts
   +-> Manual reports

2. Triage
   +-> Assess severity
   +-> Identify scope

3. Containment
   +-> Isolate affected systems
   +-> Revoke compromised tokens

4. Eradication
   +-> Remove threat
   +-> Patch vulnerabilities

5. Recovery
   +-> Restore services
   +-> Verify integrity

6. Lessons Learned
   +-> Post-incident review
   +-> Update procedures
```


## Performance Targets

| Metric | Target | Critical |
|--------|--------|----------|
| API Response (p95) | <200ms | >500ms |
| API Response (p99) | <500ms | >1000ms |
| Database Query (p95) | <50ms | >200ms |
| Throughput | 1000 req/s | <100 req/s |
| Uptime | 99.9% | <99.0% |
| Error Rate | <0.1% | >1.0% |
